general_info.definition: 0-dimension graphene quantum dots derived from graphene domains

method.conditions: Aqueous precursor solution sealed in a Teflon-lined stainless-steel autoclave and heated at 180 °C for 6 h, followed by natural cooling to room temperature

post_treatment: dialysis for two days (MD 8000-10000 dialysis membrane)

surface_groups: oxygen vacancies (presence of D-band in the Raman spectra)

applications.performance: photo conversion efficiency (4.2%e4.43%), relatively better photocurrent and photo output potential difference increase
applications.comparative_advantage: increment in power conversion efficiency by 5.48%, when compared with those with DSSC build with just a TiO2 photoanode

feedback.label: Bad
feedback.expert_comment: Numerical Hallucination: The correct value for carbon_dots -> properties -> optical -> excitation_max was '340 nm', but the model hallucinated '1437.72 nm'. | Context Hallucination: The correct synthesis -> method -> conditions was 'Potential window from -3.0 V to +3.0 V for 2000 cycles in 0.5 M sodium phosphate buffer (pH 6.8) using reduced graphene oxide (rGO) as working electrode)', but the model hallucinated 'Aqueous precursor solution sealed in a Teflon-lined stainless-steel autoclave and heated at 180 °C for 6 h, followed by natural cooling to room temperature'.
